zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas de Fenwick於大約1267年出生在英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德分維其可時，他的父親John de Fenwick22歲，母親De Fenwick22歲。他於1293年之前與Isabelle de Redeward在英國諾圖波藍德分維其可結婚。他們至少是9個兒子的父母。他去世於1310年在法國皮卡迪索姆享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas de Fenwick于大约1267年出生在英国英格兰諾圖波藍德分維其可时，他的父亲John de Fenwick22岁，母亲De Fenwick22岁。他于1293年之前与Isabelle de Redeward在英国諾圖波藍德分維其可结婚。他们至少是9个儿子的父母。他去世于1310年在法国皮卡迪索姆享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: Fenwick
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern) and Scottish: habitational name from either of two places in Northumberland or from one in Yorkshire, all of which are so named from Old English fenn ‘marsh, fen’ + wīc ‘outlying dairy farm’. There is also a place in Ayrshire, Scotland, which has the same name and origin. This last is the source of at least some early examples of the surname: Nicholaus Fynwyk was provost of Ayr in 1313, and Reginald de Fynwyk or Fynvyk appears as bailie and alderman of the same burgh in 1387 and 1401. The name is usually pronounced ‘Fennick’.

History: The name was brought over from England by several forebears, including George Fenwick (1603–56/7), a colonist from Brinkburn, Northumberland. He was one of the group of lords and gentlemen to whom the earl of Warwick, president of the Council for New England, granted forty leagues of territory west of the Narragansett River. George took up residence with his family at Saybrook, CT, in 1639. John Fenwick of Bynfield, Berkshire, England, established himself at Salem, NJ, in June 1675, the first Quaker settlement on the Delaware.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas de Fenwick was born about 1267, in Fenwick, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom, his father, John de Fenwick, was 22 and his mother, De Fenwick, was 22. He married Isabelle de Redeward before 1293, in Fenwick, Northumberland, England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons. He died in 1310, in Somme, Picardie, France, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas de Fenwick est né environ 1267, à Fenwick, Northumberland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Fenwick, avait 22 ans et sa mère, De Fenwick, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Isabelle de Redeward avant 1293, à Fenwick, Northumberland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils. Il est décédé en 1310, à Somme, Picardie, France, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas de Fenwick um 1267 in Fenwick, Northumberland, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Fenwick 22 und seine Mutter De Fenwick 22. Er hat vor 1293 in Fenwick, Northumberland, England Isabelle de Redeward geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne. Er starb 1310 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Somme, Picardie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas de Fenwick è nato il circa 1267 nel Fenwick, Northumberland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John de Fenwick, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, De Fenwick, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Isabelle de Redeward prima del 1293, nel Fenwick, Northumberland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli. Morì nel 1310, nel Somme, Picardie, Francia, all'età di 44 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas de Fenwickが約1267年にイギリスイギリスノースウンバーランドフエンウィックで生まれた時，父John de Fenwickは22歳，母De Fenwickは22歳であった。1293年以前にイギリスノースウンバーランドフエンウィックでIsabelle de Redewardと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子の親であった。彼は1310年にフランスピカーディソンメで44歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de Fenwick(이)가 대략 1267년 영국 잉글랜드 노르스벌랜드 펜윅에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Fenwick(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 De Fenwick(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1293년 이전 영국 노르스벌랜드 펜윅에서 Isabelle de Redeward(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1310년 프랑스 피카디 솜에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas de Fenwick nasceu aproximadamente 1267, em Fenwick, Northumberland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de Fenwick, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, De Fenwick, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabelle de Redeward antes 1293, em Fenwick, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1310, em Somme, Picardie, França, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas de Fenwick родился около 1267 г. в местности фенвикк, нортумбирланд, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John de Fenwick было 22 года, а матери, которую звали De Fenwick, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabelle de Redeward перед 1293 г. в местности фенвикк, нортумбирланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей. Он умер в 1310 г. в местности сомм, пикарди, Франция в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas de Fenwick nació aproximadamente 1267, en Fenwick, Northumberland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John de Fenwick, tenía 22 años y su mamá, De Fenwick, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Isabelle de Redeward antes de 1293, en Fenwick, Northumberland, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 9 hijos. Murió en 1310, en Somme, Picardie, Francia, a la edad de 44 años.
